exceptions.violation: csf/ is non-standard directory name (plugin spec says scripts/ or component dirs)
exceptions.rationale: csf/ is the primary package namespace for this project. Renaming to scripts/ would break all imports throughout the package. This is an intentional architectural decision - csf/ is a meaningful package name (Constitutional Knowledge System abbreviation), not an oversight.

exceptions.violation: bin/ is non-standard for CLI entry points without pyproject.toml entry_points declaration
exceptions.rationale: bin/ scripts use sys.path.insert hack for portability within the package directory, which breaks when pip-installed or invoked from a different working directory. The proper fix requires pyproject.toml with [project.scripts] entry points (GAP-006), which is outside PHASE 1.7 scope. This is an interim structural waiver only - full portability requires follow-up work.
